content: Just as 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems dynamically prune low-information outdated memories to prioritize storing surprising, reward-boosting experiences, general interactive agents can benefit from a curiosity-weighted memory pruning framework that reserves long-term storage only for experiences that reduce the agent’s prediction error, rather than all encountered interactions. This framework reframes general agent memory design from a capacity-constrained storage problem to an active curiosity-driven selection problem aligned with modern active learning advances.
